comment: Default-FAIL verification contract for T-002 (medium, acceptance-first). Populated after the quality gate reran tests/agent-capabilities-v2.tests.sh/.ps1 (10/10 passed both lanes) and tests/agent-model-routing.tests.sh/.ps1 (0 failed both lanes) -- AC-014's external, mechanical machine verification -- recorded together in specs/epic-159-pillar-d/verification/qg/T-002/focused-tests.log, and the epic-159-pillar-d cross-suite regression set (specs/epic-159-pillar-d/verification/qg/T-002/regression.log). Shell stack waives the three compile-oriented checks (lint/typecheck/build) for this task's JSON registry data file plus the new Markdown confirmation-record file and shell/PowerShell test-infrastructure files. Per the implementation report (reports/implementation/epic-159-pillar-d/T-002.md) and the recorded acceptance-first evidence (specs/epic-159-pillar-d/verification/T-002/pre-edit-v2-registry.json, v1-hash-before.txt, v1-hash-after.txt): the v1 registry (contracts/agent-model-capabilities.json) stayed byte-for-byte frozen across this task's edit (AC-013), and contracts/agent-model-capabilities.v2.json was updated to current-generation Anthropic/OpenAI model data with per-model supported_efforts and both hosts' effort_control paths populated (AC-012/AC-017). KNOWN, DOCUMENTED, NON-BLOCKING CONDITION (not a T-002 defect): the epic-159-pillar-d cross-suite regression set's tests/model-freshness-check.tests.sh/.ps1 lanes each show exactly one failure -- TEST-009's AC-011 live-registration assertion. tasks.md's own Commit-A Definition of Done for T-003 explicitly excepts this one assertion as a designed-red condition until a human applies specs/epic-159-pillar-d/human-copy/.github/workflows/test.yml per the epic-136 human-copy procedure (T-003's own verification contract documents this in full); it is unrelated to and unaffected by this task's own registry-data-only change, and is not cited here as a basis for withholding any of this contract's checks.

checks.waiver_reason: No lint tool configured for this repository's JSON registry data file plus the new Markdown confirmation-record file and shell/PowerShell test-infrastructure files (stack: shell); bash -n and pwsh parse validity are exercised implicitly by every green run recorded in focused-tests.log.
No static type-check command is configured for JSON registry data, Markdown, or shell/PowerShell test-infrastructure files.

No network or service integration is in scope; verification is external and mechanical against the real, unedited tests/agent-capabilities-v2.tests.sh/.ps1 and tests/agent-model-routing.tests.sh/.ps1 suites.
No compile/build toolchain for JSON registry data, Markdown, or shell/PowerShell test-infrastructure files.

No independently startable application/UI/service; this task's data-only registry edit is fully covered by the external tests/agent-capabilities-v2.tests.sh/.ps1 + tests/agent-model-routing.tests.sh/.ps1 re-runs.

No baseline-behavior.md exists for this feature; this is not a refactor/bugfix task. The change is additive data within Pillar C's C1 schema; the saved red/green evidence (v1-hash-before.txt/v1-hash-after.txt byte-identical proof) and cross-suite regression set provide the applicable behavior comparison.
No user-facing UI surface; Planned Files are a JSON registry data file, an adjacent Markdown confirmation record, and CHANGELOG.md only.
ds_profile: none; no design-system/ directory exists in this repository.
